author: y4rr
content:
Filed while closing #388. The foreach (on-device, tensor-op) update path was added to SgdConfig and AdamWConfig, but the other 17 optimizers in ferrotorch-optim still take the legacy CPU path:

- adadelta, adafactor, adagrad, adamax, adam, asgd, lbfgs, muon, nadam, natural_gradient, radam, rmsprop, rprop, sparse_adam, ema, swa, grad_accumulator, grad_scaler

The pattern from sgd.rs and adamw.rs is reusable: add a `foreach: bool` field to the Config, store moments as Tensor<T> in a parallel state map, and dispatch in step(). Each optimizer is ~150 lines of new code. The parity tests at sgd.rs and adamw.rs serve as the template — verify the new path produces identical results to the legacy path for several step counts and config combinations.

For optimizers that use Vec<f64> internally for accuracy (like AdamW does), the foreach path will compute in T precision instead — this is a documented tradeoff users opt into via foreach=true.
